invariants: User-facing product brand is mater; UI brand is mater by AMIC.
Law Firm OS, law-firm-os, package names, validators, ledgers, historical evidence, packages/matter, and matter_id are not renamed for branding.
apps/web remains the renderer source of truth until separately approved.
The desktop shell cannot bypass backend permission, audit, session, or AI policy.
File bridge access requires explicit user gesture, backend precheck, and audit evidence.
Deep links and notifications are route intents only.
Signed packaging and update work cannot imply public release.

work_packages.title: Upload, save-as, preview, and cache wipe

tuws.title: Audit current desktop and branding truth
acceptance: Current repo lists apps/web and apps/api, and explicitly records apps/desktop absence.

verification.method: find apps -maxdepth 2 -type d -print && rg -n "matter by AMIC|mater|apps/desktop" apps docs/launch
verification.evidence: Audit command output copied to LCX-DESK-00 evidence.

tuws.title: Record mater naming rules
acceptance: Naming rules classify product, UI, repo, and machine identifier layers.

tuws.title: Centralize brand constants
acceptance: Product and UI brand values are imported from one module in changed UI files.

tuws.title: Create string classification backlog
acceptance: Every planned text change is classified as product_brand, domain_object, machine_identifier, evidence, or historical.

tuws.title: Add branding validator
acceptance: Validator fails on user-facing matter brand misuse and passes domain/machine exceptions.

verification.evidence: Validator exit code and finding summary.

tuws.title: Close naming guardrail work package
acceptance: LCX-DESK-00 evidence lists commands, results, blockers, and non-claims.

tuws.title: Derive mater logo assets without deleting originals
acceptance: Existing matter brand files remain present and mater derivatives are in separate paths.

tuws.title: Port logo animation into reusable splash component
acceptance: Splash renders mater letters, AMIC byline, reduced-motion behavior, and no external runtime dependency.

tuws.title: Integrate splash into loading and auth startup surfaces
acceptance: Initial loading route and first auth surface show mater splash or logo using shared brand constants.

tuws.title: Verify branding UI build and regression
acceptance: Web build passes and UI regression includes mater startup branding.

verification.method: npm --workspace apps/web run build && npm --workspace apps/web run test:ui

tuws.title: Close branding phase evidence
acceptance: Evidence marks desktop shell, auth, and file bridge as not implemented in P0.

tuws.title: Create apps/desktop package skeleton
acceptance: Desktop package exists and does not expose file bridge, auth token storage, or update channel.

tuws.title: Implement hardened BrowserWindow defaults
acceptance: nodeIntegration=false, contextIsolation=true, sandbox=true, webSecurity=true are asserted in code and tests.

verification.method: rg -n "nodeIntegration: false|contextIsolation: true|sandbox: true|webSecurity: true" apps/desktop

tuws.permission_audit_impact: Desktop renderer gets no Node, fs, shell, path, or generic IPC authority.

tuws.title: Define dev and packaged load policy
acceptance: Only approved local dev URL and packaged file origin are allowed.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Navigation cannot load untrusted remote content into desktop shell.

tuws.title: Add desktop security validator
acceptance: Validator detects insecure BrowserWindow, missing preload allowlist, and non-allowlisted navigation.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Validator prevents authority widening before later phases.

tuws.title: Smoke desktop shell startup
acceptance: Desktop smoke proves shell starts and loads only the approved renderer target.

tuws.title: Create native splash window
acceptance: Native splash displays mater by AMIC before main renderer readiness.

tuws.title: Implement splash to main-window handoff
acceptance: Splash closes only after main renderer ready or a bounded failure fallback appears.

tuws.title: Add reduced-motion and offline fallback handling
acceptance: Reduced-motion users avoid long animation and offline startup shows non-sensitive fallback.

tuws.title: Close shell prototype evidence
acceptance: LCX-DESK-01 evidence lists shell startup, security settings, and non-claims.

tuws.title: Write OIDC PKCE session plan
acceptance: Plan bans embedded password login and renderer token persistence.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Session authority stays outside the renderer.

tuws.title: Reserve auth callback deep link route
acceptance: Auth callback route is schema-validated and cannot execute non-auth actions.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Auth callback cannot become a general action link.

tuws.title: Define secure store policy
acceptance: Policy defines token location, lifetime, logout deletion, and tenant switch invalidation.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Token and tenant material are not written to renderer localStorage/sessionStorage.

tuws.title: Define logout and cache wipe contract
acceptance: Contract names all cache classes that must be wiped on logout and tenant switch.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Tenant data cannot survive logout or tenant switch.

tuws.title: Implement main-process auth coordinator
acceptance: Auth coordinator performs PKCE flow without exposing tokens to renderer.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Renderer receives session status only.

tuws.title: Expose minimal preload session API
acceptance: Preload API exposes session state and commands only; no generic ipcRenderer is exported.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Renderer cannot access generic IPC or token bodies.

tuws.title: Test token absence and cache cleanup
acceptance: Tests prove token absence in renderer storage and cache deletion on logout.

verification.method: npm --workspace apps/desktop run test:session && rg -n "access_token|refresh_token" apps/desktop/src apps/web/src
verification.evidence: Session test output plus token grep.

tuws.permission_audit_impact: No token material remains in renderer code paths.

tuws.title: Close auth/session evidence
acceptance: Evidence records auth commands, token grep, logout/cache cleanup, and remaining blockers.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Session closure remains evidence-bound.

tuws.title: Map desktop route intents to web routes
acceptance: Route map covers matter, document, task, auth, denied, and fallback routes.

tuws.title: Implement denied-state adapter
acceptance: Denied state hides row counts, snippets, citations, and document metadata for unauthorized matter/document access.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: UI reflects backend denial without leaking hidden metadata.

tuws.title: Expose desktop runtime context without authority
acceptance: Renderer can read desktop mode and route source, but cannot mutate files, auth, audit, or billing state.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Desktop context is presentation-only.

tuws.title: Add unauthorized matter visibility tests
acceptance: Tests prove non-member matter row, count, snippet, and citation are absent.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Permission trimming is visible in UI tests.

tuws.title: Smoke deep-link permission recheck
acceptance: A mater://matter route rechecks backend permission before screen entry.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Deep link cannot bypass server-owned authorization.

tuws.title: Run desktop workspace UI regression
acceptance: Matter, Vault, denied, and desktop mode surfaces stay routable.

verification.method: npm --workspace apps/web run build && npm --workspace apps/web run test:ui

tuws.title: Close workspace evidence
acceptance: Evidence includes happy path, denied path, and no desktop authority claim.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Workspace closeout documents permission boundaries.

tuws.title: Finalize desktop file bridge contract
acceptance: Contract allows choose_file_for_upload, save_document_as, open_temp_preview, and clear_temp_cache only.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Contract blocks silent folder scan, broad watch, arbitrary path read/write, and long-term path retention.

tuws.title: Implement user gesture file picker guard
acceptance: File selection only occurs through a user-triggered picker path.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: No silent disk scan is possible.

tuws.title: Add backend precheck and audit mapping
acceptance: Upload/download/save-as paths require backend permission precheck and audit event mapping.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Every file bridge operation is audit-bound.

tuws.title: Add no silent scan static guard
acceptance: Validator fails on directory watch, recursive scan, arbitrary path read, arbitrary path write, and path retention patterns.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Local disk access remains narrow and inspectable.

tuws.title: Implement choose-file-for-upload handler
acceptance: Upload handler returns file handle metadata needed by backend only after user selection and permission precheck.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Upload cannot read arbitrary paths.

tuws.title: Implement save-document-as handler
acceptance: Save-as uses a save dialog and never writes to a default path silently.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Downloads are explicit and audit-bound.

tuws.title: Implement temp preview and cache wipe
acceptance: Temp preview files are scoped, time-bounded, and removed on logout, tenant switch, and app quit.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Document body cache does not persist across session boundaries.

tuws.title: Run complete file bridge test suite
acceptance: Happy path, denied path, no silent scan, and cache wipe tests all pass.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: File bridge evidence includes allow and deny behavior.

tuws.title: Close file bridge evidence
acceptance: Evidence records picker-only access, precheck, audit, cache wipe, and forbidden boundary checks.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Evidence makes local file authority explicit.

tuws.title: Finalize deep link contract
acceptance: Contract allows matter, document, task, and auth_callback route intents only.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Links cannot execute mutation/download/upload/AI/billing actions.

tuws.title: Implement deep link parser
acceptance: Parser validates scheme, route type, identifier shape, and unknown parameter rejection.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Malformed links fail closed.

tuws.title: Deny action execution from links
acceptance: Mutation, download, upload, AI generation, billing write, and delivery execution links are rejected.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Deep links stay read-route only.

tuws.title: Add permission and audit on link open
acceptance: Opening a valid route records an open audit and rechecks backend permission.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Route entry is permission-bound.

tuws.title: Define generic notification policy
acceptance: Policy forbids client names, matter names, document names, snippets, deadlines with sensitive labels, and billing amounts on lock screen.

tuws.title: Implement notification click route intent
acceptance: Notification click passes only a route intent through the deep-link parser.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Notification cannot execute privileged actions.

tuws.title: Audit notification sensitive text
acceptance: Validator fails if notification templates include sensitive data placeholders.

tuws.title: Close deep link and notification evidence
acceptance: Evidence records route-only behavior, denylist tests, and notification sensitive-copy audit.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Evidence keeps route and notification authority explicit.

tuws.title: Record internal app ID decision
acceptance: Decision separates internal app ID from any future public release app ID.

tuws.title: Add packaging configuration
acceptance: Packaging config includes app ID, product name, icons, files, and no public publish channel.

tuws.title: Produce macOS internal signed build
acceptance: macOS build receipt records signing identity, notarization state, and install smoke result.

verification.evidence: macOS internal build output and receipt.

tuws.title: Produce Windows internal signed build
acceptance: Windows build receipt records signing identity, installer hash, and install smoke result.

verification.evidence: Windows internal build output and receipt.

tuws.title: Define signed update channel policy
acceptance: Policy defines internal, pilot, rollback, disabled-public, and key rotation rules.

tuws.title: Smoke signed update and rollback
acceptance: Update smoke proves signature check and rollback path.

tuws.title: Run SBOM and license audit
acceptance: Audit lists new desktop dependencies, licenses, and redistribution constraints.

verification.evidence: Dependency tree and license audit output.

tuws.title: Close packaging and update evidence
acceptance: Evidence states signed internal build status and explicitly denies public release approval.

tuws.title: Run pilot QA checklist
acceptance: QA-01 through QA-12 have pass, fail, blocked, or owner-risk status with evidence links.

tuws.title: Observe install, launch, login, logout, and cache wipe
acceptance: Manual QA records install, launch, login, logout, tenant switch, and cache wipe receipts.

verification.method: manual QA: record screenshots, command receipts, and cache paths checked

tuws.permission_audit_impact: Pilot session evidence proves token/cache cleanup.

tuws.title: Observe file bridge, deep link, and notification QA
acceptance: Manual QA covers allow and deny paths for file bridge, deep link, and notifications.

verification.method: manual QA: run QA-06 through QA-09 and attach receipts

tuws.permission_audit_impact: Native capabilities remain bounded in pilot evidence.

tuws.title: Run security hardening audit
acceptance: Audit checks Electron settings, preload allowlist, token absence, file bridge guards, deep link denylist, and notification copy.
tuws.permission_audit_impact: Security audit is the phase gate for pilot go/no-go.

tuws.title: Adjudicate pilot risk register
acceptance: Every failed or blocked QA item has owner, severity, mitigation, and go/no-go impact.

tuws.title: Prepare owner decision packet
acceptance: Packet separates repo-ready, pilot-ready, production-ready, public-release, and owner-approved states.

tuws.title: Assemble final desktop evidence index
acceptance: Index links LCX-DESK-00 through LCX-DESK-07 evidence files and every phase terminal TUW.

tuws.title: Add no-public-release guard
acceptance: Validator fails if desktop evidence claims public release, production go-live, or owner approval without matching receipt.

tuws.title: Close pilot evidence and go/no-go boundary
acceptance: Evidence records final status, owner decision state, residual risks, and explicit non-claims.
